event: Satan watching the endearments of Adam and Eve
symbols: two nude figures embracing — Adam and Eve in the innocence of Eden before the Fall
observing figure (Satan) — the fallen angel watching with envy and plotting temptation
garden setting suggested by foliage — the Garden of Eden, humanity's original home
description: A rough pencil sketch showing two nude figures — a man and a woman — standing close together in an embrace. The figures are loosely drawn with faint, flowing lines. Surrounding foliage and cloudy forms are sketched around them. The drawing is very light and preliminary, with the artist's handwriting reading 'Adam & Eve' at the bottom. The style is characteristic of William Blake's visionary preparatory drawings, with an emphasis on the human form and spiritual atmosphere rather than realistic detail.
theological_description: This sketch captures the moment described in Milton's Paradise Lost (Book IV) when Satan, expelled from heaven, first beholds the bliss of Adam and Eve in Eden and is consumed with jealous rage. While the subject is literary, its theological roots are deeply biblical. Satan's envy of human happiness reflects the patristic tradition that the devil's fall was precipitated by jealousy of humanity's creation in God's image. The Wisdom of Solomon states: 'Through the devil's envy death entered the world' (WIS.2.24). The innocent embrace of Adam and Eve before the Fall represents humanity as God intended — 'naked and not ashamed' (GEN.2.25), living in complete transparency before God and each other. Satan's voyeuristic observation introduces the note of threat that will culminate in the serpent's temptation (GEN.3.1-5). The scene embodies the fundamental biblical drama: innocence observed by malice, goodness stalked by evil.

scripture_refs.reason: Adam and Eve naked and not ashamed — their pre-Fall innocence depicted in the embrace
The serpent's temptation that Satan's watching here anticipates
Through the devil's envy death entered the world — Satan's jealousy motivating his observation
The fall of Lucifer from heaven, the backstory of the watching Satan
